---
bibliography: references.bib
---

# Executive Summary

**{duckspatial}** and **{duckh3}** are two R packages that bring DuckDB's spatial and H3 analytical engines into R through a lazy, memory-efficient interface. They allow R users to analyse spatial datasets that exceed available RAM — reading directly from disk-resident files, executing geometry operations and H3 indexing inside DuckDB, and returning results to R only when explicitly requested. Both packages are already on CRAN. {duckspatial} (v1.0.0) was first released in April 2025 and receives ~820 downloads per month. {duckh3} was released in April 2026 following the same design philosophy.

This proposal requests **$4,576** over 12 months to fund:

- Complete documentation with worked examples for all exported functions in both packages.
- A vignette suite (≥5 per package) covering real-world spatial workflows.
- Test coverage raised to ≥85% in both packages.
- New spatial functions completing the {duckspatial} analytical toolkit.
- Expanded {dplyr} verb support for `duckspatial_df` lazy spatial frames.
- Explore visualization support for `duckspatial_df` objects.
- Explore the support of the `raster` extension in a new package.
- A reproducible performance benchmark vignette.
- Ongoing maintenance against new DuckDB releases throughout the grant period.

Work will be communicated through quarterly R Consortium blog posts, release announcements, YouTube videos, and a planned Udemy course.

The primary beneficiaries are R users — ecologists, spatial data scientists, urban analysts, and engineers — who currently have no practical alternative for large-scale spatial analysis within the R ecosystem.
